stories.headline: SEC's Atkins teases tokenization sandbox via 'innovation exemptions' window
stories.tldr: SEC Chair Paul Atkins said the agency will launch innovation exemptions in the coming weeks, letting firms pilot on-chain tokenization and securitization under a regulated carve-out.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A formal SEC sandbox is the first credible path for U.S. issuers to tokenize equities and funds without a Howey landmine — and it pulls forward institutional product launches by quarters.
the_details: Atkins announced the framework live at Bitcoin 2026 in Las Vegas, alongside CFTC and White House officials in a coordinated 'unified approach' message.
Senator Lummis simultaneously pinned the CLARITY Act for an August advance, sequencing the legislative track behind the SEC's exemption launch.
White House Presidential Advisory Council on Digital Assets flagged a separate Strategic Bitcoin Reserve announcement also coming in 'the coming weeks.'

stories.headline: Four central banks set rates this week — BOJ, Fed, ECB, BOE all on deck
stories.tldr: BOJ, Fed, ECB and BOE all hand down policy decisions this week, the densest cluster of major-economy rate prints crypto has faced in 2026.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: BTC's correlation to dollar moves has hit its most extreme negative in nearly four years — a hawkish Fed Wednesday could detonate the leveraged long book that built up around the $80K reclaim.
the_details: Markets are pricing the Fed to hold at 3.50%–3.75% on April 29; any hawkish-hold language reprices crypto risk premia immediately.
Kevin Warsh takes the Fed chair on May 15, so this is Powell's final FOMC — adding lame-duck communication risk on top of the decision itself.
BTC sold off nearly 3% to under $77,000 on April 28 on geopolitics and rate jitters, after failing to break the $80,000 ceiling this weekend.

stories.headline: Crypto ETF AUM jumps to $155B as BTC funds soak up $933M weekly inflow
stories.tldr: Total crypto ETF AUM has rebounded to $155 billion — the highest reading since February — driven by a $933 million weekly inflow into Bitcoin funds.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Sidelined institutional capital is back on the bid even as spot BTC slips below $77K, signalling that ETF allocators see this dip as accumulation, not exit.
the_details: Onchain data shows billions in fresh stablecoin deposits hitting exchanges alongside the ETF inflows, a classic dry-powder setup ahead of the FOMC.
Goldman Sachs filed for its first Bitcoin ETF product earlier in April, deepening the traditional-finance bench beyond BlackRock and Fidelity.
Concentrated sell orders capped the rally near $80,000 — analysts flagged the level as the next breakout trigger if FOMC clears risk.

stories.headline: TOKEN2049 Dubai postponed to 2027 as UAE security risks scrap May event
stories.tldr: TOKEN2049 organizers pushed the Dubai conference from May 2026 to 2027, citing heightened security risks across the UAE.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Dubai has been the unofficial off-site capital of the bull-cycle conference circuit — losing it for a year reroutes deal-flow and BD calendars to Singapore and the U.S. through 2026.
the_details: The cancellation lands the same week the U.S.–Iran ceasefire ended, putting Gulf-region risk back in the macro frame for crypto events.
The Singapore edition of TOKEN2049 in October 2026 is now the next major industry-wide gathering on the calendar.
Sponsors and side-event hosts who had committed Dubai budgets are scrambling to redeploy spend toward Bitcoin 2026 Vegas and Token2049 SG.

stories.headline: Jupiter unlocks $9.67M JUP — 1.54% of supply — into a fragile altcoin tape
stories.tldr: Solana DEX aggregator Jupiter releases 1.54% of circulating supply, worth $9.67 million in JUP, on April 28 amid a fading altcoin season.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The unlock hits while the CMC Altcoin Season Index sits at 39/100 firmly in 'Bitcoin Season,' meaning weakened bid depth could amplify any dump from early backers.
the_details: Bitcoin dominance has climbed to 60% in April 2026, draining liquidity from mid-cap altcoins like JUP into BTC and ETF-driven majors.
Solana spot ETF inflows already collapsed 92% from their $419M peak to just $34M in April, removing one of JUP's macro tailwinds.
Bittensor (TAO) is the standout altcoin counter-trend, up 47% YTD with $43M in Q1 revenue, drawing capital that might otherwise rotate into JUP.
